fields.description:
## Context / Problem

The final Jira modules still sit at the package root: `acli.py`, `acli_cli_ops.py`,
`acli_graph.py`, `acli_rest.py`, `acli_subprocess.py`, `adf.py`, `outbound_fields.py`, and
`comment_limits.py`. Per ADR 0035 Â§(c) the seam is `adapters/jira/`, so these belong under it.
Two constraints (ADR Â§1/Â§2) make this the **last** story and force it to be atomic: (1) `adf.py`,
`outbound_fields.py`, and `comment_limits.py` are **location-pinned** â€” loaded by filename via
`_loader.lazy_load` / `spec_from_file_location`, so moving them breaks the loader unless the
loader is taught the new sub-package dir in the same change; (2) `acli*` has a **~29-test patch
surface** â€” every `mock.patch("rebar_reconciler.acliâ€¦")` / `patch.object` string must be
rewritten in the same commit, with no re-export shim (ADR Decision 4). After S4
[rebar:6673-7636-a116-4f90] the core no longer imports these modules, so only the adapter's own
imports, the loader, and the test patch strings need updating â€” which is what makes the move
safe to do now.

Edit-set:
- Physically move the eight modules into `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/`.
- Teach the loader the new directory: update `_loader.lazy_load` / `__main__._load_sibling_keyed`
  and the `spec_from_file_location(...)` sites that name `adf.py` / `outbound_fields.py` /
  `comment_limits.py` to resolve under `adapters/jira/`.
- Update every internal importer of the moved modules to the new canonical path (one binding
  site each; **no re-export shim** at the old path).
- Rewrite the ~29 test patch strings (`rebar_reconciler.acli*` â†’ `â€¦adapters.jira.acli*`, and
  the `adf`/`outbound_fields`/`comment_limits` patch strings) atomically in the same change.

## Approach

Single atomic change (the constraints forbid a partial move). Move the files, update the loader
so location-pinned lookups resolve in `adapters/jira/`, repoint importers, and rewrite all
patch strings â€” then run the full suite. Because S4 already removed the core's direct imports,
the residual references are a closed, enumerable set (`grep` for each old module path across
`src/` and `tests/`); the change is complete when that grep returns zero hits at the old paths
and the suite is green. No behavior changes â€” this is a pure relocation + rebind.

## Scope

- Move the eight remaining Jira modules into `adapters/jira/`.
- Loader updates for the three location-pinned modules.
- Importer repointing + atomic test-patch-string rewrite (~29), no shims.
- **Out of scope:** any behavior change; the core rewire (S4, prerequisite); new backends
  (epic `be74`). `jira_fields.py` already moved in Phase 1 â€” untouched here.

## Testing

- **Tier:** integration (full suite) + a structural gate, RED-first where applicable.
- **Full suite green** after the move is the primary oracle â€” the ~29 patched tests exercise
  the moved modules at their new paths.
- **Loader test:** a test that dynamically loads `adf`/`outbound_fields`/`comment_limits`
  through the reconciler entry point succeeds against the new location (would fail RED if the
  loader were not taught the new dir â€” verified by staging the move without the loader edit).
- **Structural gate:** `grep` finds **zero** references to the old root paths of the moved
  modules in `src/` and `tests/`, and **no** re-export shim file remains at any old path.
- **Teeth:** temporarily leave one patch string at the old path and confirm that test errors
  (proving the patch strings genuinely bind the moved modules).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The eight modules physically reside under
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/`; no source file remains at their old root
  paths and no re-export shim exists there â€” verified by `ls`/`grep`.
- [ ] The file-location loader resolves `adf` / `outbound_fields` / `comment_limits` at the new
  sub-package path (verified by the loader test through the reconciler entry point).
- [ ] All ~29 test patch strings reference the new `adapters.jira.*` paths; `grep` for the old
  `rebar_reconciler.acli*` / root-`adf`/`outbound_fields`/`comment_limits` patch targets returns
  zero hits â€” verified by `grep`.
- [ ] Every `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/*.py` stays within the 800-LOC module-size cap
  (`.github/module-size-limit.txt`) after the move â€” verified by the module-size gate.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all green.
